props.question: Do Washington and Tehran both publicly confirm a signed memorandum by 16 June?

agents.name: Sprockett
agents.why: The draft terms are granular and mediators are still moving, but Sunday certainty has been replaced by Iranian permission. Marginal yes.

agents.name: Tinkerton
agents.why: Israel struck the Lebanese file Iran made part of the bargain, and Ghalibaf publicly questioned US enforcement. Tehran now has an elegant reason to wait; Trump can declare it done, the system need not countersign his calendar.

no.note: The dissenter is the designated one. Read the gap, not the consensus.

items.note: Trump says OPEN TO ALL and a Sunday signing; Iran says no final decision. A ceasefire written as a logistics memo — and tankers still run at a tenth of normal.
Israel killed a Hezbollah commander hours before the signing claim; Hezbollah fired three projectiles. Ghalibaf: Washington 'lacks the will or the ability.' The clause added from outside the room.
Gray-zone pressure continues — ADIZ incursions and coast-guard patrols — but no kinetic incident before the 30 June window.
Houthi shipping threats keep war-risk and rerouting alive as a conditional second front to any Gulf deal.

items.what: A US export-control directive forces Anthropic to disable its top models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5, for all users — the first time capability-as-a-service, not chips, is the controlled good.
SpaceX closes its first week public at a ~$2.1T valuation on a 3-4% float; S&P declines to fast-track it, saying the company meets none of its criteria.
Brent settles at $87.33, down 3.4% as the war premium fades on deal headlines — even as Hormuz tanker traffic runs near a tenth of normal.
The DOJ clears Paramount-Skydance's ~$110B purchase of Warner Bros Discovery as 'unlikely to harm competition'; California and New York signal a joint challenge.
The 48-team World Cup opens to thousands of empty seats at Levi's Stadium and $1,890 group-stage tickets; FIFA defends a claimed 99% attendance by counting the concourse.
The Knicks beat the Spurs 94-90 in Game 5 to win their first NBA title since 1973, ending a 53-year drought.
Swiss voters reject a referendum to cap the population at 10 million, avoiding a clash with EU free movement.
Trump says US forces killed Tren de Aragua leader 'Niño Guerrero' in Venezuela — gang as military target, not criminal network.
